title: centaurea atrata subsp. nigrofimbria
intro: Centaurea atrata subsp. nigrofimbria, also known as Black-stemmed Knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea atrata subsp. nigrofimbria include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
